question: Count all flights in the flight table grouped by departure_airport code
sql:
SELECT 
    f.departure_airport, 
    COUNT(f.flight_id) AS flight_count
FROM 
    postgres_air.flight as f
JOIN 
    postgres_air.airport as a ON f.departure_airport = a.airport_code
GROUP BY 
    f.departure_airport;

question: Count all flights in the flight table grouped by departure_airport of AAH and ABJ
sql:
SELECT f.departure_airport, a.airport_name, COUNT(f.flight_id) AS flight_count 
FROM postgres_air.flight as f 
JOIN postgres_air.airport as a ON f.departure_airport = a.airport_code 
WHERE departure_airport IN ('AAH', 'ABJ')
GROUP BY f.departure_airport, a.airport_name;

question: how many number of distinct cites are there
sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT flight.aircraft_code) 
FROM postgres_air.flight
JOIN postgres_air.aircraft ON flight.aircraft_code = aircraft.code
WHERE flight.status IN ('scheduled', 'en-route'); -- replace with your actual condition

-- For number of distinct cities where airports are located
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT airport.city) 
FROM postgres_air.flight
JOIN postgres_air.airport ON flight.arrival_airport = airport.airport_code OR flight.departure_airport = airport.airport_code
WHERE flight.status IN ('scheduled', 'en-route'); -- replace with your actual condition

question: how many airport are there in each city
sql:
SELECT city, COUNT(DISTINCT flight.departure_airport) as airport_count
FROM postgres_air.airport
JOIN postgres_air.flight ON flight.departure_airport = airport.airport_code
GROUP BY city;

count passengers whose age is lesse than 18

FROM postgres_air.account AS a
JOIN postgres_air.passenger AS p ON a.account_id = p.account_id

question: count of distinct sear in boarding pass
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT boarding_pass.seat) as num_searches FROM postgres_air.boarding_pass;

question: which Which airport has the most departures airport
sql:
SELECT COUNT(flight.flight_id) as flight_count, airport.airport_code, airport.airport_name
FROM postgres_air.flight 
INNER JOIN postgres_air.airport ON flight.departure_airport = airport.airport_code
WHERE flight.departure_airport IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY airport.airport_code, airport.airport_name
ORDER BY flight_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Who are the top 5 most frquent flyers passengers
sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) as passenger_count, postgres_air.account.*
FROM postgres_air.frequent_flyer
JOIN postgres_air.passenger ON postgres_air.passenger.account_id = postgres_air.account.account_id 
WHERE postgres_air.frequent:30007612-d5b6-4a9c-83f2-2cb58cb6e91f, postgres_air.account.account_id = 'None' AND postgres_air.frequent_flyer.award_points > 1
GROUP BY postgres_air.account.account_id
ORDER BY passenger_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Who are the top 5 most frquent flyers passengers
sql:
SELECT passenger.*, COUNT(booking.*) AS bookings_count FROM postgres_air.passenger
LEFT JOIN postgres_air.booking ON passenger.booking_ref = booking.booking_ref 
WHERE age > 21
GROUP BY passenger.id, passenger.name, passenger.date_of_birth, passenger.age, passenger.gender, passenger.phone_number, passenger.email
ORDER BY bookings_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Who are the top 5 most frquent flyers passengers
sql:
SELECT p.passenger_id, p.first_name, p.last_name, COUNT(bp.passenger_id) AS flight_count 
FROM postgres_air.passenger p 
JOIN postgres_air.boarding_pass bp ON p.passenger_id = bp.passenger_id 
GROUP BY p.passenger_id 
ORDER BY flight_count DESC 
LIMIT 5;

question: Count flights departing from each airport
sql:
SELECT f.departure_airport, COUNT(flight_id) AS flight_count 
FROM postgres_air.flight AS f 
JOIN postgres_air.airport AS a ON f.departure_airport = a.airport_code 
WHERE status = 'scheduled' 
GROUP BY departure_airport;

question: Show distinct departure_airport in the flight table
Find passengers who have taken more than 3 flights
sql:
SELECT f.first_name, f.last_name, COUNT(*) as flight_count
FROM postgres_air.passenger p
JOIN postgres_air.boarding_pass bp ON p.passenger_id = bp.passenger_id
JOIN postgres_air.booking_leg bl ON bp.booking_leg_id = bl.booking_leg_id
JOIN postgres_air.flight f ON bl.flight_id = f.flight_id
WHERE p.age > 18 AND f.scheduled_departure > p.update_ts
GROUP BY f.first_name, f.last_name
ORDER BY flight_count DESC;

question: Find flights that have more than 50 passengers
sql:
SELECT 
    passenger.first_name, 
    passenger.last_name, 
    COUNT(flight.flight_id) as delayed_flights_count 
FROM 
    "postgres_air"."flight" 
JOIN 
    "postgres_air"."booking" ON flight.flight_id = booking.booking_id 
JOIN 
    "postgres_air"."passenger" ON booking.passenger_id = passenger.passenger_id 
WHERE 
    flight.status = 'Delayed' AND MONTH(flight.scheduled_departure) = MONTH(NOW() + INTERVAL '1 month') 
    AND flight.arrival_airport = airport.airport_code 
GROUP BY 
    passenger.first_name, 
    passenger.last_name;
error:

LINE 10:     "postgres_air"."passenger" ON booking.passenger_id = pas...
                                           ^

question: Find passengers who have taken more than 3 flights
sql:
SELECT p.passenger_id, p.first_name, p.last_name
FROM "postgres_air"."passenger" AS p
JOIN "postgres_air"."boarding_pass" AS bp ON p.passenger_id = bp.passenger_id
GROUP BY p.passenger_id, p.first_name, p.last_name
HAVING COUNT(bp.passenger_id) > 3;

question: Find flights that have more than 50 passengers
sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS passenger_count, f.flight_id
FROM "postgres_air"."flight" as f
LEFT JOIN "postgres_air"."aircraft" as a ON f.aircraft_code = a.code
JOIN "postgres_air"."booking_leg" as bl ON f.flight_id = bl.flight_id
JOIN "postgres_air"."boarding_pass" as bp ON bl.booking_leg_id = bp.booking_leg_id
GROUP BY f.flight_id 
HAVING COUNT(bp.passenger_id) > 50;

question: Count passengers for each departure_airport
sql:
SELECT f.departure_airport, COUNT(DISTINCT b.passenger_id) as num_passengers
FROM postgres_air.flight f
JOIN postgres_air.airport a ON a.airport_code = f.departure_airport
JOIN postgres_air.booking b ON b.flight_id = f.flight_id
GROUP BY f.departure_airport
LIMIT 10;
error:

                                       ^
HINT:  Perhaps you meant to reference the column "f.flight_id".

question: Count passengers for each departure_airport
sql:
SELECT 
    flight.departure_airport,
    COUNT(passenger.passenger_id) as passenger_count
FROM 
    postgres_air.flight
JOIN 
    postgres_air.booking_leg ON flight.flight_id = booking_leg.flight_id
JOIN 
    postgres_air.boarding_pass ON booking_leg.booking_id = boarding_pass.booking_leg_id
JOIN 
    postgres_air.passenger ON boarding_pass.passenger_id = passenger.passenger_id
WHERE 
    flight.departure_airport = 'LAX' AND flight.arrival_airport = 'DEN'
GROUP BY 
    flight.departure_airport
LIMIT 10;

question: count passenger in each of departure_aiport flights
sql:
SELECT f.departure_airport, COUNT(p.passenger_id) as num_passengers
FROM postgres_air.flight as f
JOIN postgres_air.airport as a ON f.departure_airport = a.airport_code
JOIN postgres_air.booking_leg as bl ON bl.flight_id = f.flight_id
JOIN postgres_air.passenger as p ON p.booking_id = bl.booking_id
GROUP BY f.departure_airport
LIMIT 10;

question: which aircraft used most flights connected with aircraft_code
sql:
SELECT 
        flight.*
    FROM postgres_air.flight
    WHERE departure_airport = 'JFK' AND (arrival_airport = 'GRU' OR arrivalarrival_airport = 'AUS')
), flight AS (
    SELECT 
        flight.*, aircraft.model
    FROM flight
    JOIN postgres_air.aircraft ON flight.aircraft_code = aircraft.code
)
SELECT 
    departure_airport, arrival_airport, model, COUNT(*) as flight_count
FROM flight
GROUP BY departure_airport, arrival_airport, model
LIMIT 10;

question: which aircraft used most flights connected with aircraft_code
sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS flight_count, aircraft.code
FROM postgres_air.flight
JOIN postgres_air.aircraft ON flight.aircraft_code = aircraft.code
GROUP BY aircraft.code
LIMIT 10;

question: names of all passengers on flight id 100
sql:
SELECT passenger.first_name, passenger.last_name
FROM postgres_air."flight"
JOIN postgres_air."booking_leg" ON flight.flight_id = booking_leg.flight_id 
JOIN postgres_air."passenger" ON booking_leg.booking_id = passenger.booking_id
WHERE flight.flight_id = 100
LIMIT 10;

question: Count passengers for each departure_airport
sql:
SELECT 
    flight.departure_airport,
    COUNT(passenger.passenger_id) AS num_passengers
FROM 
    postgres_air.flight
JOIN 
    postgres_air.booking_leg ON flight.flight_id = booking_leg.flight_id
JOIN 
    postgres_air.passenger ON booking_leg.booking_id = passenger.booking_id
GROUP BY 
    flight.departure_airport
LIMIT 10;

question: Count total aircraft
sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT flight.flight_id) as total_aircrafts
FROM postgres_air."flight"
JOIN postgres_air."booking_leg" ON flight.flight_id = booking_leg.flight_id 
JOIN postgres_air."passenger" ON booking_leg.booking_id = passenger.booking_id
LIMIT 10;

question: Count flights for each aircraft model
sql:
SELECT 
    COUNT(f.flight_id) AS flight_count,
    a.model
FROM 
    postgres_air.flight as f
JOIN 
    postgres_air.aircraft as a ON f.aircraft_code = a.code
GROUP BY 
    a.model
ORDER BY 
    flight_count DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: COunt top 5 bookings done by which account and how many
sql:
SELECT a.account_id, COUNT(*) as total_passengers
FROM postgres_air."booking_leg" bl
JOIN postgres_air."passenger" p on bl.booking_id = p.booking_id 
JOIN postgres_air."account" a on p.account_id = a.account_id 
GROUP BY a.account_id
ORDER BY total_passengers DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Find accounts that have more than 5 bookings
sql:
SELECT SUM(price) FROM postgres_air.booking INNER JOIN postgres_air.account ON booking.account_id = account.account_id WHERE account.first_name = 'John' AND account.last_name = 'Doe'
LIMIT 10;

question: Find flights that have more than 50 passengers.
sql:
SELECT flight.*, COUNT(passenger.passenger_id) as passenger_count
FROM postgres_air."flight"
JOIN postgres_air."booking_leg" ON flight.flight_id = booking_leg.flight_id 
JOIN postgres_air."passenger" ON booking_leg.booking_id = passenger.booking_id
GROUP BY flight.flight_id, flight.flight_no, flight.scheduled_departure, flight.scheduled_arrival, 
         flight.departure_airport, flight.arrival_airport, flight.status, flight.aircraft_code, 
         flight.actual_departure, flight.actual_arrival, flight.update_ts
HAVING COUNT(passenger.passenger_id) > 50
LIMIT 10;

question: Find the airport with the highest number of arrivals
sql:
SELECT f.departure_airport, COUNT(*) as num_passengers
FROM postgres_air."flight" f
JOIN postgres_air."booking_leg" bl on bl.flight_id = f.flight_id
JOIN postgres_air."passenger" p on p.booking_id = bl.booking_id
GROUP BY f.departure_airport
ORDER BY num_passengers DESC
LIMIT 1;
